message: Task started: Create Practice Pages Specification Documentation

details.text: [0m⚙ [0mcodegraph_codegraph_explore {"query":"Reading, Listening, Writing, Speaking, Grammar, Vocabulary practice pages IELTS","maxFiles":15}
Now I'll create the practice pages specification document based on the thorough codebase analysis.
- **Listening Practice** — Audio player with waveform + speed control, synchronized transcript, timestamped notes, table completion mobile UX, AI Tutor explanation of transcript

- **Writing Practice** — Prompt browser, focused writing view with phase-based timer (Plan/Write/Review), band score circle with target comparison, 4-criterion AI feedback, diff-highlighted improved version

- **Speaking Practice** — Part-based question browser, full-screen recording panel with waveform, self-evaluation sliders, AI scores vs self scores comparison, cue card display, speech-to-text integration

- **Shared patterns** — Common browse→practice→results→history flow, consistent empty/loading/error states, skill-specific color coding (reading=blue, listening=purple, writing=green, speaking=orange, grammar=pink, vocabulary=teal), AI Tutor integration at every point
- **Reading Practice** — Browse/passage view (2-column reading + question panel), results with per-question review, vocabulary extraction from text, AI Tutor explanation of passages/questions
- **Grammar Practice** — 3-tab layout (Topics/Exercises/Weaknesses), status badges (weak/reviewing/mastered), exercise flow with detailed answer explanations, skill-linked grammar topics

- **Vocabulary Practice** — Session configuration (mode/word count/difficulty/topic), flashcard review with spaced repetition ratings (Again/Hard/Good/Easy), streak counter, AI Tutor memory tricks and related words

- **6 main sections** — Connection Status Card, Install Extension, How It Helps (6 feature cards), Sync & Data Flow, Data Import, Troubleshooting
- **Primary/secondary actions** — Install, Sync Now, Import Vocabulary, Import Articles, Export, Refresh, Troubleshoot
- **Empty/loading/error states** — Not installed, not connected, no data to import, previously connected, sync failed, bridge error
- **Design notes** — Inspired by the Dribbble reference using teal/cyan gradients matching the existing codebase extension theme
- **Components needed** — 7 new components (ConnectionStatusCard, InstallBrowserCard, ExtensionFeatureCard, SyncFlowDiagram, ImportProgressModal, TroubleshootingAccordion, ConnectionBanner)
- **Accessibility** — Status badges with `role="status"`, accordion `aria-expanded`, specific `aria-label` on install buttons
- **Mobile layout & responsive behavior** — Breakpoint-specific layouts, accordion-based feature cards, compact status
- **AI Tutor integration** — Feature explanations, troubleshooting AI, smart import suggestions
- **Proposed layouts** — Desktop (connected, not connected, error), mobile (compact accordion-based)

message: Task started: Create Empty, Loading, and Error States Specification Documentation

details.text: Now I have a clear picture of the documentation style. Let me create the Empty, Loading, and Error States specification document.

- **Loading State Patterns** (6 types): 10 domain-specific page skeletons (Dashboard, TaskList, VocabList, Chat, Practice, etc.), section skeletons, button loading, AI generation progress (with chunked/partial support), and typing indicator
- **Retry Patterns** (4 types): automatic retry, manual retry button, pull-to-refresh (mobile), dismissible errors

- **Special States**: offline/local-first (banner + local storage fallback), AI generation lifecycle (in-progress/complete/failed/partial), not enough data, logged-out, session expired, deleted/not found content
- **Error State Patterns** (8 types): page-level, section-level/inline, network/offline, AI generation failed, API rate limit, permission/auth, form validation, and empty search
- **State Transition Rules**: loading→content, loading→error, error→retry, empty→first content with timing expectations
- **Empty State Patterns** (5 types): page-level, section-level/inline, first-time user, search/filter, and a comprehensive context-specific message table for 22+ features
- **Implementation Notes**: existing components to enhance, `PageState<T>` pattern, new components needed (`OfflineBanner`, `GenerationProgress`)
- **Design Principle**: never show a blank page — every state has a friendly message, explanation, and clear actionable next step
- **Accessibility**: full ARIA roles table, focus management per transition, contrast ratios, `prefers-reduced-motion` support, screen reader announcements

- Full accessibility checklist for mobile (touch targets, screen readers, color contrast, reduced motion)
- Modal/drawer mobile patterns (bottom sheet with drag handle, snap points)
- Form input guidelines (48px height, 16px font for iOS zoom prevention)
- Anti-patterns to avoid (hover menus, tiny targets, 6+ bottom nav items, < 16px inputs)

message: Task started: Create Accessibility Specification Documentation

details.text: Now let me check the responsive mobile spec and an example page spec for style consistency:
Now I have a clear understanding of the documentation style. Let me create the accessibility specification:
The document covers all required areas:
| **Focus States** | Focus ring design, per-element states, `:focus-visible`, `:focus-within` for cards |
| **Color Contrast** | Ratio requirements per WCAG AA/AAA, token verification pairs, dark mode, forced colors |
| **Keyboard Navigation** | Tab order, keyboard shortcuts, focus management per pattern, roadmap keyboard nav |
| **Button/Input Accessibility** | Labels, states, types, association, error linking, search, checkbox, select, slider |
| **Semantic HTML** | Document structure, heading hierarchy, landmark regions, lists, buttons vs links |
| **Error Message Clarity** | Principles, format, per-scenario examples, form validation, error accessibility |
| **Don't Rely on Color** | Non-color indicators for all status/skill/difficulty, skill icons, chart accessibility, color blindness |
| **Testing Checklist** | Automated (axe, Lighthouse) and manual (VoiceOver, NVDA, TalkBack, keyboard-only) |
| **Screen Reader Labels** | ARIA patterns, announcements for key events, dynamic content, `sr-only` utility |
| **Mobile Touch Size** | 44x44px minimums, touch spacing, safe areas, gesture alternatives |
| **Component-Specific** | Bottom nav, AI Tutor chat, flashcards, study plan timeline, charts, modals, tabs |
| **Redesign-Specific** | What the redesign improves, mobile-first accessibility benefits, AI Tutor accessibility |
The spec follows the same structure and tone as the other design documentation files in `docs/design/`.
| **Localization** | Language attribute, RTL support, multi-language navigation |
